message:
test(fcp-conformance/mesh-gossip): add MSH-GS-6 freshness window + MSH-GS-5 removal coverage (br-bzahl)

mesh_gossip_summary_conformance.rs covered determinism, single-byte
tamper detection, cross-peer payload equality, and object-addition
detection — but had no test for GossipSummary::is_stale (whose
docstring explicitly cites a closed regression where future-dated
summaries used to slip past the 'age > ttl_secs' check because
saturating_sub returned 0 for any future timestamp).

Adds:

- msh_gs_5_differs_from_flags_object_removal — symmetric digest
  difference on object removal (existing MSH-GS-5 only tested addition;
  a digest implementation that handled additions but ignored removals
  would silently fail to reconcile shrinking zones).
- msh_gs_6_is_stale_false_for_summary_within_ttl
- msh_gs_6_is_stale_true_for_summary_past_ttl
- msh_gs_6_is_stale_false_for_summary_within_future_skew
- msh_gs_6_is_stale_true_for_summary_beyond_future_skew (regression
  guard: pinned the saturating_sub-zero future-timestamp bypass)
- msh_gs_6_is_stale_true_for_summary_far_in_past_zero_skew

All 11 tests pass (5 original + 6 new). Verified locally via
cargo test -p fcp-conformance --test mesh_gossip_summary_conformance.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
